summary.explanation: Federal comment periods sometimes receive duplicate submissions — revised resubmissions, organizational comments also filed under personal names, or identical form letters counted twice. We identified 7 duplicate submissions in the original 305 comments. These are excluded from our statistical analysis to avoid double-counting. The original submissions remain accessible below and on regulations.gov for full transparency.

pairs.detail: Same commenter submitted substantially identical comment (~17,000 characters) six days later. The earlier submission (0036) is retained.

kept.commenter: North American Association of Central Cancer Registries
kept.organization: North American Association of Central Cancer Registries

pairs.detail: 97.8% identical text. The organizational submission (NAACCR, 0108) is retained; the personal-name filing (0110) by a likely staff member is removed.
Same commenter, same day, revised resubmission with near-identical text. The earlier submission (0153) is retained.
Same organization, same day, revised resubmission with near-identical text (~15,600 characters). The earlier submission (0215) is retained.
Same company filed under slightly different names ('Clinical Architecture LLC' vs 'Clinical Architecture'). Identical themes and sentiment. The full legal name submission (0228) is retained.
Both are blank/attachment-only submissions with identical fields, submitted on the same day. Technical duplicate from the regulations.gov system. The earlier submission (0300) is retained.

100% identical text submitted by different individuals. This appears to be a form letter template. Only one instance is counted to avoid inflating the totals; the earlier filing (Barg-Walkow, 0147) is retained.
